entries.content: I'll read the spec file and create an implementation plan with phases and subtasks.

entries.detail:
1→# Safety Check-in & Location Sharing
     2→
     3→Safety-focused check-in system with automated check-ins, emergency contacts, real-time location sharing with trusted contacts, check-in scheduling, and missed check-in alerts. Includes emergency SOS functionality and status updates.
     4→
     5→## Rationale
     6→Addresses critical user pain point: 'Safety concerns when traveling solo without trusted contacts knowing your location.' Also addresses location sharing privacy concerns (pain-7-1, pain-7-2) by implementing privacy-first approach. This is a key differentiator from general travel apps.
     7→
     8→## User Stories
     9→- As a solo traveler, I want to share my location with trusted contacts so that someone knows where I am in case of emergency
    10→- As a safety-conscious user, I want automated check-ins so that my contacts are alerted if I don't report in safely
    11→- As a traveler, I want an emergency SOS button so that I can quickly get help if needed
    12→- As a privacy-conscious user, I want to control exactly who sees my location and when
    13→
    14→## Acceptance Criteria
    15→- [ ] Users can designate trusted contacts from their phone or app community
    16→- [ ] Users can schedule automatic check-ins at specific times or locations
    17→- [ ] Trusted contacts receive location updates and status notifications
    18→- [ ] Missed check-ins trigger alerts to trusted contacts with last known location
    19→- [ ] Emergency SOS button shares real-time location with all trusted contacts
    20→- [ ] Users can manually update their status (safe, need help, emergency)
    21→- [ ] Location sharing is granular - users control what, when, and with whom
    22→- [ ] Battery-efficient location tracking prevents excessive drain
    23→

    26→/// Base class for all exceptions in the app
    27→abstract class AppException implements Exception {

    35→

    41→    required String message,
    42→    required this.statusCode,
    43→  }) : super(message);
    44→}
    45→

1→{
     2→  "feature": "Safety Check-in & Location Sharing",
     3→  "description": "# Safety Check-in & Location Sharing

Safety-focused check-in system with automated check-ins, emergency contacts, real-time location sharing with trusted contacts, check-in scheduling, and missed check-in alerts. Includes emergency SOS functionality and status updates.

## Rationale
Addresses critical user pain point: 'Safety concerns when traveling solo without trusted contacts knowing your location.' Also addresses location sharing privacy concerns (pain-7-1, pain-7-2) by implementing privacy-first approach. This is a key differentiator from general travel apps.

## User Stories
- As a solo traveler, I want to share my location with trusted contacts so that someone knows where I am in case of emergency
- As a safety-conscious user, I want automated check-ins so that my contacts are alerted if I don't report in safely
- As a traveler, I want an emergency SOS button so that I can quickly get help if needed
- As a privacy-conscious user, I want to control exactly who sees my location and when

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Users can designate trusted contacts from their phone or app community
- [ ] Users can schedule automatic check-ins at specific times or locations
- [ ] Trusted contacts receive location updates and status notifications
- [ ] Missed check-ins trigger alerts to trusted contacts with last known location
- [ ] Emergency SOS button shares real-time location with all trusted contacts
- [ ] Users can manually update their status (safe, need help, emergency)
- [ ] Location sharing is granular - users control what, when, and with whom
- [ ] Battery-efficient location tracking prevents excessive drain
",

entries.content:
I've successfully created a comprehensive implementation plan for the **Safety Check-in & Location Sharing** feature. Here's what I've set up:

## Implementation Plan Summary

Created a **10-phase plan with 41 subtasks** covering:

### Phase 1-2: Foundation (Domain & Data Layer)
- Domain entities (TrustedContact, CheckIn, LocationUpdate, SafetyAlert, SafetyStatus)
- Data models with JSON serialization
- Repository interfaces and use cases
- Local/remote data sources with GraphQL integration

### Phase 3: Core Services
- Battery-efficient location tracking using geolocator
- Background check-in service using workmanager
- Notification service for reminders and alerts
- Missed check-in detector
- Platform configuration for Android/iOS

### Phase 4: State Management
- Riverpod states and notifiers
- Provider setup for dependency injection

### Phase 5-8: UI Implementation
- **Trusted Contacts**: List, add/edit, picker, card widgets
- **Check-ins**: Home, manual, scheduler, history screens
- **Emergency SOS**: SOS screen, status updates, prominent SOS button
- **Location Sharing**: Management screens with privacy controls

### Phase 9: Integration
- Safety hub as main entry point
- Navigation routes
- App navigation integration
- Quick SOS button on home screen

### Phase 10: Testing
- Unit tests for domain and data layers
- Widget tests for UI components
- Integration tests for complete flows

## Key Technical Decisions

- Follows existing **clean architecture pattern** (like auth feature)
- Uses **Riverpod** for state management
- **Battery-efficient** location tracking
- **Privacy-first** approach for location sharing
- **Offline support** with graceful degradation
- **Workmanager** for background check-ins
- **GraphQL** for API communication

The plan is now saved and ready for implementation. All phases follow the existing project structure and patterns, ensuring consistency with the codebase.
I'll help you create the entities for the safety check-in and location sharing feature. Let me start by reading the spec, plan, and context files to understand the requirements.

1→import 'package:equatable/equatable.dart';
     2→
     3→/// User entity representing an authenticated user
     4→class User extends Equatable {
     5→  /// Unique identifier for the user
     6→  final String id;
     7→
     8→  /// User's email address
     9→  final String email;
    10→
    11→  /// User's username
    12→  final String username;
    13→
    14→  /// When the user was created
    15→  final DateTime createdAt;
    16→
    17→  /// When the user last logged in
    18→  final DateTime? lastLoginAt;
    19→
    20→  /// The access token for AWS resources
    21→  final String? accessToken;
    22→
    23→  /// The ID token containing user claims
    24→  final String? idToken;
    25→
    26→  /// The refresh token for getting new access/ID tokens
    27→  final String? refreshToken;
    28→
    29→  /// When the tokens expire
    30→  final DateTime? tokenExpiresAt;
    31→
    32→  /// Creates a new [User] instance
    33→  const User({
    34→    required this.id,
    35→    required this.email,
    36→    required this.username,
    37→    required this.createdAt,
    38→    this.lastLoginAt,
    39→    this.accessToken,
    40→    this.idToken,
    41→    this.refreshToken,
    42→    this.tokenExpiresAt,
    43→  });
    44→
    45→  @override
    46→  List<Object?> get props => [
    47→        id,
    48→        email,
    49→        username,
    50→        createdAt,
    51→        lastLoginAt,
    52→        accessToken,
    53→        idToken,
    54→        refreshToken,
    55→        tokenExpiresAt,
    56→      ];
    57→
    58→  /// Creates a copy of this user with the given fields replaced with new values
    59→  User copyWith({
    60→    String? id,
    61→    String? email,
    62→    String? username,
    63→    DateTime? createdAt,
    64→    DateTime? lastLoginAt,
    65→    String? accessToken,
    66→    String? idToken,
    67→    String? refreshToken,
    68→    DateTime? tokenExpiresAt,
    69→  }) {
    70→    return User(
    71→      id: id ?? this.id,
    72→      email: email ?? this.email,
    73→      username: username ?? this.username,
    74→      createdAt: createdAt ?? this.createdAt,
    75→      lastLoginAt: lastLoginAt ?? this.lastLoginAt,
    76→      accessToken: accessToken ?? this.accessToken,
    77→      idToken: idToken ?? this.idToken,
    78→      refreshToken: refreshToken ?? this.refreshToken,
    79→      tokenExpiresAt: tokenExpiresAt ?? this.tokenExpiresAt,
    80→    );
    81→  }
    82→
    83→  /// Creates an empty user
    84→  factory User.empty() {
    85→    return User(
    86→      id: '',
    87→      email: '',
    88→      username: '',
    89→      createdAt: DateTime.now(),
    90→      lastLoginAt: null,
    91→    );
    92→  }
    93→
    94→  /// Whether this user is empty (not authenticated)
    95→  bool get isEmpty => id.isEmpty;
    96→
    97→  /// Whether this user is not empty (authenticated)
    98→  bool get isNotEmpty => !isEmpty;
    99→
   100→  /// Whether the user has valid tokens
   101→  bool get hasValidTokens =>
   102→      accessToken != null &&
   103→      idToken != null &&
   104→      tokenExpiresAt != null &&
   105→      DateTime.now().isBefore(tokenExpiresAt!);
   106→
   107→  /// Time until token expiration
   108→  Duration? get timeUntilTokenExpiry =>
   109→      tokenExpiresAt?.difference(DateTime.now());
   110→
   111→  @override
   112→  bool operator ==(Object other) {
   113→    if (identical(this, other)) return true;
   114→
   115→    return other is User &&
   116→        other.id == id &&
   117→        other.email == email &&
   118→        other.username == username &&
   119→        other.createdAt == createdAt &&
   120→        other.lastLoginAt == lastLoginAt &&
   121→        other.accessToken == accessToken &&
   122→        other.idToken == idToken &&
   123→        other.refreshToken == refreshToken &&
   124→        other.tokenExpiresAt == tokenExpiresAt;
   125→  }
   126→
   127→  @override
   128→  int get hashCode {
   129→    return id.hashCode ^
   130→        email.hashCode ^
   131→        username.hashCode ^
   132→        createdAt.hashCode ^
   133→        lastLoginAt.hashCode ^
   134→        accessToken.hashCode ^
   135→        idToken.hashCode ^
   136→        refreshToken.hashCode ^
   137→        tokenExpiresAt.hashCode;
   138→  }
   139→
   140→  @override
   141→  String toString() {
   142→    return 'User{id: $id, email: $email, username: $username, createdAt: $createdAt, lastLoginAt: $lastLoginAt, hasValidTokens: $hasValidTokens}';
   143→  }
   144→}
   145→

entries.content: Great! The commit was successful. Now let me update the implementation plan to mark this subtask as completed:

entries.detail:
1→{
     2→  "feature": "Safety Check-in & Location Sharing",
     3→  "description": "# Safety Check-in & Location Sharing\n\nSafety-focused check-in system with automated check-ins, emergency contacts, real-time location sharing with trusted contacts, check-in scheduling, and missed check-in alerts. Includes emergency SOS functionality and status updates.\n\n## Rationale\nAddresses critical user pain point: 'Safety concerns when traveling solo without trusted contacts knowing your location.' Also addresses location sharing privacy concerns (pain-7-1, pain-7-2) by implementing privacy-first approach. This is a key differentiator from general travel apps.\n\n## User Stories\n- As a solo traveler, I want to share my location with trusted contacts so that someone knows where I am in case of emergency\n- As a safety-conscious user, I want automated check-ins so that my contacts are alerted if I don't report in safely\n- As a traveler, I want an emergency SOS button so that I can quickly get help if needed\n- As a privacy-conscious user, I want to control exactly who sees my location and when\n\n## Acceptance Criteria\n- [ ] Users can designate trusted contacts from their phone or app community\n- [ ] Users can schedule automatic check-ins at specific times or locations\n- [ ] Trusted contacts receive location updates and status notifications\n- [ ] Missed check-ins trigger alerts to trusted contacts with last known location\n- [ ] Emergency SOS button shares real-time location with all trusted contacts\n- [ ] Users can manually update their status (safe, need help, emergency)\n- [ ] Location sharing is granular - users control what, when, and with whom\n- [ ] Battery-efficient location tracking prevents excessive drain\n",

entries.content:
Perfect! Let me create a summary of what was completed:

## Summary

I've successfully completed **Subtask 1.1: Create domain entities for safety feature**. Here's what was implemented:

### Created Domain Entities

1. **TrustedContact** (`lib/features/safety/domain/entities/trusted_contact.dart`)
   - Represents trusted emergency contacts
   - Includes enums: `ContactSource` (phone, community) and `ContactPermission` (emergencyOnly, checkIns, fullAccess)
   - Fields: id, userId, name, phoneNumber, email, source, communityUserId, permission, locationSharingEnabled, receivesCheckIns, receivesEmergencyAlerts, addedAt, updatedAt, revokedAt, notes

2. **CheckIn** (`lib/features/safety/domain/entities/check_in.dart`)
   - Represents check-ins (manual and scheduled)
   - Includes nested entity: `CheckInLocation`
   - Enums: `CheckInStatus` (scheduled, active, completed, missed, cancelled) and `CheckInTriggerType` (manual, scheduledTime, locationArrival, locationDeparture)
   - Fields: id, userId, triggerType, status, scheduledTime, deadline, completedAt, location, statusMessage, tripId, notifyContactIds, alertSent, alertSentAt, metadata, createdAt, updatedAt

3. **LocationUpdate** (`lib/features/safety/domain/entities/location_update.dart`)
   - Represents location updates shared with trusted contacts
   - Enum: `LocationSharingStatus` (active, paused, ended)
   - Fields: id, userId, latitude, longitude, accuracy, altitude, speed, heading, address, placeName, sharingStatus, sharedWithContactIds, batteryLevel, isEmergency, emergencyAlertId, checkInId, metadata, createdAt

4. **SafetyAlert** (`lib/features/safety/domain/entities/safety_alert.dart`)
   - Represents safety alerts sent to trusted contacts
   - Includes nested entity: `SafetyAlertLocation`
   - Enums: `SafetyAlertType` (emergencySOS, needHelp, emergency, missedCheckIn, locationUpdate, safe) and `SafetyAlertStatus` (sent, acknowledged, resolved, cancelled)
   - Fields: id, userId, type, status, message, location, notifiedContactIds, acknowledgedByContactIds, triggeredAt, firstAcknowledgedAt, resolvedAt, cancelledAt, batteryLevel, checkInId, tripId, metadata, createdAt, updatedAt

5. **SafetyStatus** (`lib/features/safety/domain/entities/safety_status.dart`)
   - Represents the user's current safety status
   - Includes nested entity: `SafetyStatusLocation`
   - Enum: `SafetyStatusType` (safe, needHelp, emergency, unknown)
   - Fields: id, userId, status, message, location, batteryLevel, timestamp, updatedAt, safetyAlertId, checkInId, metadata

### Key Design Decisions

- **Freezed Pattern**: All entities use Freezed for immutability, automatic copyWith, and code generation
- **Nested Entities**: Location data is modeled as nested entities for reusability
- **Comprehensive Enums**: Each entity uses appropriate enums to represent states and types
- **JSON Serialization**: All entities support JSON serialization via fromJson factory methods
- **Privacy-First**: Granular permission levels for trusted contacts (emergencyOnly, checkIns, fullAccess)
- **Offline Support**: Entities include all necessary metadata for offline-first operation

### Next Steps

**Note**: The Freezed code generation files (`.freezed.dart` and `.g.dart`) will need to be generated when build tools are available by running:
```bash
dart run build_runner build --delete-conflicting-outputs
```

The implementation plan and build progress have been updated, and changes have been committed to git.
I'll help you implement this subtask. Let me start by reading the spec and plan to understand the context.

entries.detail:
1→# Safety Check-in & Location Sharing
     2→
     3→Safety-focused check-in system with automated check-ins, emergency contacts, real-time location sharing with trusted contacts, check-in scheduling, and missed check-in alerts. Includes emergency SOS functionality and status updates.
     4→
     5→## Rationale
     6→Addresses critical user pain point: 'Safety concerns when traveling solo without trusted contacts knowing your location.' Also addresses location sharing privacy concerns (pain-7-1, pain-7-2) by implementing privacy-first approach. This is a key differentiator from general travel apps.
     7→
     8→## User Stories
     9→- As a solo traveler, I want to share my location with trusted contacts so that someone knows where I am in case of emergency
    10→- As a safety-conscious user, I want automated check-ins so that my contacts are alerted if I don't report in safely
    11→- As a traveler, I want an emergency SOS button so that I can quickly get help if needed
    12→- As a privacy-conscious user, I want to control exactly who sees my location and when
    13→
    14→## Acceptance Criteria
    15→- [ ] Users can designate trusted contacts from their phone or app community
    16→- [ ] Users can schedule automatic check-ins at specific times or locations
    17→- [ ] Trusted contacts receive location updates and status notifications
    18→- [ ] Missed check-ins trigger alerts to trusted contacts with last known location
    19→- [ ] Emergency SOS button shares real-time location with all trusted contacts
    20→- [ ] Users can manually update their status (safe, need help, emergency)
    21→- [ ] Location sharing is granular - users control what, when, and with whom
    22→- [ ] Battery-efficient location tracking prevents excessive drain
    23→

<system-reminder>
Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.
</system-reminder>

entries.content: Now I'll read the domain entities to understand what needs to be serialized, and check the pattern files to understand the code conventions.
Let me read the safety domain entities and auth model patterns to understand the structure:

entries.detail:
1→import 'package:freezed_annotation/freezed_annotation.dart';
     2→
     3→part 'trusted_contact.freezed.dart';
     4→part 'trusted_contact.g.dart';
     5→
     6→/// Source of the trusted contact (phone contacts or app community)
     7→enum ContactSource {
     8→  phone,
     9→  community,
    10→}
    11→
    12→/// Permission level for trusted contacts
    13→enum ContactPermission {
    14→  /// Can only receive alerts during emergencies
    15→  emergencyOnly,
    16→  /// Can receive check-ins and emergency alerts
    17→  checkIns,
    18→  /// Can receive check-ins, emergency alerts, and location updates
    19→  fullAccess,
    20→}
    21→
    22→/// Represents a trusted emergency contact
    23→@freezed
    24→class TrustedContact with _$TrustedContact {
    25→  const factory TrustedContact({
    26→    /// Unique identifier for the trusted contact
    27→    required String id,
    28→
    29→    /// User ID who owns this trusted contact
    30→    required String userId,
    31→
    32→    /// Contact's name
    33→    required String name,
    34→
    35→    /// Contact's phone number
    36→    required String phoneNumber,
    37→

1→import 'package:soloadventurer/features/auth/domain/entities/user.dart';
     2→
     3→/// Data layer representation of [User] entity
     4→class UserModel extends User {
     5→  const UserModel({
     6→    required super.id,
     7→    required super.email,
     8→    required super.username,
     9→    required super.createdAt,
    10→    super.lastLoginAt,
    11→  });
    12→
    13→  /// Creates a [UserModel] from JSON map
    14→  factory UserModel.fromJson(Map<String, dynamic> json) {
    15→    return UserModel(
    16→      id: json['id'] as String,
    17→      email: json['email'] as String,
    18→      username: json['username'] as String,
    19→      createdAt: DateTime.parse(json['created_at'] as String),
    20→      lastLoginAt: json['last_login_at'] != null
    21→          ? DateTime.parse(json['last_login_at'] as String)
    22→          : null,
    23→    );
    24→  }
    25→
    26→  /// Converts this [UserModel] to JSON map
    27→  Map<String, dynamic> toJson() {
    28→    return {
    29→      'id': id,
    30→      'email': email,
    31→      'username': username,
    32→      'created_at': createdAt.toIso8601String(),
    33→      'last_login_at': lastLoginAt?.toIso8601String(),
    34→    };
    35→  }
    36→
    37→  /// Creates a [UserModel] from a [User] entity
    38→  factory UserModel.fromEntity(User user) {
    39→    return UserModel(
    40→      id: user.id,
    41→      email: user.email,
    42→      username: user.username,
    43→      createdAt: user.createdAt,
    44→      lastLoginAt: user.lastLoginAt,
    45→    );
    46→  }
    47→}

1→{
     2→  "feature": "Safety Check-in & Location Sharing",
     3→  "description": "# Safety Check-in & Location Sharing\n\nSafety-focused check-in system with automated check-ins, emergency contacts, real-time location sharing with trusted contacts, check-in scheduling, and missed check-in alerts. Includes emergency SOS functionality and status updates.\n\n## Rationale\nAddresses critical user pain point: 'Safety concerns when traveling solo without trusted contacts knowing your location.' Also addresses location sharing privacy concerns (pain-7-1, pain-7-2) by implementing privacy-first approach. This is a key differentiator from general travel apps.\n\n## User Stories\n- As a solo traveler, I want to share my location with trusted contacts so that someone knows where I am in case of emergency\n- As a safety-conscious user, I want automated check-ins so that my contacts are alerted if I don't report in safely\n- As a traveler, I want an emergency SOS button so that I can quickly get help if needed\n- As a privacy-conscious user, I want to control exactly who sees my location and when\n\n## Acceptance Criteria\n- [ ] Users can designate trusted contacts from their phone or app community\n- [ ] Users can schedule automatic check-ins at specific times or locations\n- [ ] Trusted contacts receive location updates and status notifications\n- [ ] Missed check-ins trigger alerts to trusted contacts with last known location\n- [ ] Emergency SOS button shares real-time location with all trusted contacts\n- [ ] Users can manually update their status (safe, need help, emergency)\n- [ ] Location sharing is granular - users control what, when, and with whom\n- [ ] Battery-efficient location tracking prevents excessive drain\n",

1→# Safety Check-in & Location Sharing - Build Progress
     2→
     3→## Project Status: IN PROGRESS
     4→
     5→Implementation of Phase 1 has begun.
     6→
     7→## Completed Subtasks
     8→
     9→### Phase 1: Core Data Models & Domain Layer
    10→
    11→✅ **Subtask 1.1: Create domain entities** (COMPLETED)
    12→- Created TrustedContact entity with enums for ContactSource and ContactPermission
    13→- Created CheckIn entity with nested CheckInLocation and enums for CheckInStatus and CheckInTriggerType
    14→- Created LocationUpdate entity with enum for LocationSharingStatus
    15→- Created SafetyAlert entity with nested SafetyAlertLocation and enums for SafetyAlertType and SafetyAlertStatus
    16→- Created SafetyStatus entity with nested SafetyStatusLocation and enum for SafetyStatusType

    18→- Entities include comprehensive documentation and support JSON serialization

    89→### Key Technical Details

    91→- **Architecture**: Clean Architecture (Domain → Data → Infrastructure → Presentation)

Perfect! Subtask 1.2 has been successfully completed. Here's a summary of what was implemented:

## ✅ Subtask 1.2 Completed: Create Data Models for JSON Serialization

### Files Created:
1. **trusted_contact_model.dart** - Model for TrustedContact entity
2. **check_in_model.dart** - Model for CheckIn entity with nested CheckInLocationModel
3. **location_update_model.dart** - Model for LocationUpdate entity
4. **safety_alert_model.dart** - Model for SafetyAlert entity with nested SafetyAlertLocationModel
5. **safety_status_model.dart** - Model for SafetyStatus entity with nested SafetyStatusLocationModel

### Key Features:
- ✅ All models extend their corresponding domain entities
- ✅ Handle **snake_case ↔ camelCase** conversion for JSON serialization
- ✅ Comprehensive enum serialization/deserialization for:
  - ContactSource, ContactPermission
  - CheckInStatus, CheckInTriggerType
  - LocationSharingStatus
  - SafetyAlertType, SafetyAlertStatus
  - SafetyStatusType
- ✅ **fromJson()** constructors for JSON deserialization
- ✅ **toJson()** methods for JSON serialization
- ✅ **fromEntity()** factories for entity-to-model conversion
- ✅ **toEntity()** methods for model-to-entity conversion
- ✅ Follows the existing project pattern (UserModel reference)

### Commits:
- `4e243e9` - Model implementation
- `dbbdc08` - Plan update

### Next Steps:
Subtask 1.3: Define repository interfaces (SafetyRepository interface)
I'll help you create the SafetyRepository interface. Let me start by reading the specification and plan to understand the requirements.

1→import 'package:freezed_annotation/freezed_annotation.dart';
     2→
     3→part 'trusted_contact.freezed.dart';
     4→part 'trusted_contact.g.dart';
     5→
     6→/// Source of the trusted contact (phone contacts or app community)
     7→enum ContactSource {
     8→  phone,
     9→  community,
    10→}
    11→
    12→/// Permission level for trusted contacts
    13→enum ContactPermission {
    14→  /// Can only receive alerts during emergencies
    15→  emergencyOnly,
    16→  /// Can receive check-ins and emergency alerts
    17→  checkIns,
    18→  /// Can receive check-ins, emergency alerts, and location updates
    19→  fullAccess,
    20→}
    21→
    22→/// Represents a trusted emergency contact
    23→@freezed
    24→class TrustedContact with _$TrustedContact {
    25→  const factory TrustedContact({
    26→    /// Unique identifier for the trusted contact
    27→    required String id,
    28→
    29→    /// User ID who owns this trusted contact
    30→    required String userId,
    31→
    32→    /// Contact's name
    33→    required String name,
    34→
    35→    /// Contact's phone number
    36→    required String phoneNumber,
    37→
    38→    /// Contact's email (optional)
    39→    String? email,
    40→
    41→    /// Source of the contact (phone or community)
    42→    required ContactSource source,
    43→
    44→    /// Community user ID if from community source
    45→    String? communityUserId,
    46→
    47→    /// Permission level for this contact
    48→    required ContactPermission permission,
    49→
    50→    /// Whether location sharing is currently active with this contact
    51→    @Default(false) bool locationSharingEnabled,
    52→
    53→    /// Whether this contact receives check-in notifications
    54→    @Default(true) bool receivesCheckIns,
    55→
    56→    /// Whether this contact receives emergency alerts
    57→    @Default(true) bool receivesEmergencyAlerts,
    58→
    59→    /// When this contact was added
    60→    required DateTime addedAt,
    61→
    62→    /// When this contact was last updated
    63→    DateTime? updatedAt,
    64→
    65→    /// When this trusted contact relationship was revoked (if applicable)
    66→    DateTime? revokedAt,
    67→
    68→    /// Notes about this contact
    69→    String? notes,
    70→  }) = _TrustedContact;
    71→
    72→  factory TrustedContact.fromJson(Map<String, dynamic> json) =>
    73→      _$TrustedContactFromJson(json);
    74→}
    75→

entries.detail:
1→{
     2→  "feature": "Safety Check-in & Location Sharing",
     3→  "description": "# Safety Check-in & Location Sharing\n\nSafety-focused check-in system with automated check-ins, emergency contacts, real-time location sharing with trusted contacts, check-in scheduling, and missed check-in alerts. Includes emergency SOS functionality and status updates.\n\n## Rationale\nAddresses critical user pain point: 'Safety concerns when traveling solo without trusted contacts knowing your location.' Also addresses location sharing privacy concerns (pain-7-1, pain-7-2) by implementing privacy-first approach. This is a key differentiator from general travel apps.\n\n## User Stories\n- As a solo traveler, I want to share my location with trusted contacts so that someone knows where I am in case of emergency\n- As a safety-conscious user, I want automated check-ins so that my contacts are alerted if I don't report in safely\n- As a traveler, I want an emergency SOS button so that I can quickly get help if needed\n- As a privacy-conscious user, I want to control exactly who sees my location and when\n\n## Acceptance Criteria\n- [ ] Users can designate trusted contacts from their phone or app community\n- [ ] Users can schedule automatic check-ins at specific times or locations\n- [ ] Trusted contacts receive location updates and status notifications\n- [ ] Missed check-ins trigger alerts to trusted contacts with last known location\n- [ ] Emergency SOS button shares real-time location with all trusted contacts\n- [ ] Users can manually update their status (safe, need help, emergency)\n- [ ] Location sharing is granular - users control what, when, and with whom\n- [ ] Battery-efficient location tracking prevents excessive drain\n",

1→# Safety Check-in & Location Sharing - Build Progress

     3→## Project Status: IN PROGRESS

     5→Implementation of Phase 1 has begun.

     7→## Completed Subtasks

    10→
    11→✅ **Subtask 1.1: Create domain entities** (COMPLETED)
    12→- Created TrustedContact entity with enums for ContactSource and ContactPermission
    13→- Created CheckIn entity with nested CheckInLocation and enums for CheckInStatus and CheckInTriggerType

Perfect! The task has been completed successfully. Here's a summary of what was accomplished:

## Summary

### ✅ Subtask 1.3: Create SafetyRepository Interface - COMPLETED

I've successfully created the **SafetyRepository interface** with comprehensive safety-related operations at:
`lib/features/safety/domain/repositories/safety_repository.dart`

#### Key Features Implemented:

**1. Trusted Contacts Operations (5 methods)**
- addTrustedContact, removeTrustedContact, updateTrustedContact
- getTrustedContacts, getTrustedContact

**2. Check-in Operations (8 methods)**
- createCheckIn, completeCheckIn, scheduleCheckIn, cancelCheckIn
- getUpcomingCheckIns, getAllCheckIns, getCheckIn, getCheckInsByTrip
- updateCheckInStatus

**3. Location Sharing Operations (6 methods)**
- shareLocation, stopLocationSharing, stopAllLocationSharing
- getActiveLocationShares, getLocationUpdates
- updateLocationSharingPermission

**4. Emergency SOS Operations (4 methods)**
- triggerEmergencySOS, updateSafetyStatus
- getSafetyStatus, getSafetyStatusForUser

**5. Safety Alerts Operations (7 methods)**
- getSafetyAlerts, getSafetyAlert, getRecentSafetyAlerts
- acknowledgeSafetyAlert, resolveSafetyAlert, cancelSafetyAlert
- getMissedCheckInAlerts

**6. Battery & Location Services (2 methods)**
- updateBatteryLevel, getBatteryLevel

**7. Settings & Preferences (3 methods)**
- updateContactNotificationPreferences
- getSafetySettings, updateSafetySettings

#### Code Quality:
- ✅ Follows existing repository patterns (AuthRepository, ProfileRepository)
- ✅ Uses proper Dart naming conventions
- ✅ All methods return Future types for async operations
- ✅ Comprehensive documentation comments
- ✅ Clean Architecture principles (Domain layer abstraction)
- ✅ No console.log or debug statements
- ✅ Proper imports of all entity types

#### Commits:
1. `5c047e6` - feat(safety): create SafetyRepository interface
2. `ac2ac4d` - chore: update plan and progress for subtask 1.3

The implementation plan and build progress have been updated to reflect the completion of this subtask. The next step would be **Subtask 1.4: Create use cases for trusted contacts**.
I'll help you implement the trusted contacts management use cases. Let me start by reading the specification and understanding the codebase structure.

entries.detail:
1→# Safety Check-in & Location Sharing
     2→
     3→Safety-focused check-in system with automated check-ins, emergency contacts, real-time location sharing with trusted contacts, check-in scheduling, and missed check-in alerts. Includes emergency SOS functionality and status updates.
     4→
     5→## Rationale
     6→Addresses critical user pain point: 'Safety concerns when traveling solo without trusted contacts knowing your location.' Also addresses location sharing privacy concerns (pain-7-1, pain-7-2) by implementing privacy-first approach. This is a key differentiator from general travel apps.
     7→
     8→## User Stories
     9→- As a solo traveler, I want to share my location with trusted contacts so that someone knows where I am in case of emergency
    10→- As a safety-conscious user, I want automated check-ins so that my contacts are alerted if I don't report in safely
    11→- As a traveler, I want an emergency SOS button so that I can quickly get help if needed
    12→- As a privacy-conscious user, I want to control exactly who sees my location and when
    13→
    14→## Acceptance Criteria
    15→- [ ] Users can designate trusted contacts from their phone or app community
    16→- [ ] Users can schedule automatic check-ins at specific times or locations
    17→- [ ] Trusted contacts receive location updates and status notifications
    18→- [ ] Missed check-ins trigger alerts to trusted contacts with last known location
    19→- [ ] Emergency SOS button shares real-time location with all trusted contacts
    20→- [ ] Users can manually update their status (safe, need help, emergency)
    21→- [ ] Location sharing is granular - users control what, when, and with whom
    22→- [ ] Battery-efficient location tracking prevents excessive drain
    23→

entries.detail:
1→import 'package:freezed_annotation/freezed_annotation.dart';
     2→
     3→part 'trusted_contact.freezed.dart';
     4→part 'trusted_contact.g.dart';
     5→
     6→/// Source of the trusted contact (phone contacts or app community)
     7→enum ContactSource {
     8→  phone,
     9→  community,
    10→}
    11→
    12→/// Permission level for trusted contacts
    13→enum ContactPermission {
    14→  /// Can only receive alerts during emergencies
    15→  emergencyOnly,
    16→  /// Can receive check-ins and emergency alerts
    17→  checkIns,
    18→  /// Can receive check-ins, emergency alerts, and location updates
    19→  fullAccess,
    20→}
    21→
    22→/// Represents a trusted emergency contact
    23→@freezed
    24→class TrustedContact with _$TrustedContact {
    25→  const factory TrustedContact({
    26→    /// Unique identifier for the trusted contact
    27→    required String id,
    28→
    29→    /// User ID who owns this trusted contact
    30→    required String userId,
    31→
    32→    /// Contact's name
    33→    required String name,
    34→
    35→    /// Contact's phone number
    36→    required String phoneNumber,
    37→
    38→    /// Contact's email (optional)
    39→    String? email,
    40→
    41→    /// Source of the contact (phone or community)
    42→    required ContactSource source,
    43→
    44→    /// Community user ID if from community source
    45→    String? communityUserId,
    46→
    47→    /// Permission level for this contact
    48→    required ContactPermission permission,
    49→
    50→    /// Whether location sharing is currently active with this contact
    51→    @Default(false) bool locationSharingEnabled,
    52→
    53→    /// Whether this contact receives check-in notifications
    54→    @Default(true) bool receivesCheckIns,
    55→
    56→    /// Whether this contact receives emergency alerts
    57→    @Default(true) bool receivesEmergencyAlerts,
    58→
    59→    /// When this contact was added
    60→    required DateTime addedAt,
    61→
    62→    /// When this contact was last updated
    63→    DateTime? updatedAt,
    64→
    65→    /// When this trusted contact relationship was revoked (if applicable)
    66→    DateTime? revokedAt,
    67→
    68→    /// Notes about this contact
    69→    String? notes,
    70→  }) = _TrustedContact;
    71→
    72→  factory TrustedContact.fromJson(Map<String, dynamic> json) =>
    73→      _$TrustedContactFromJson(json);
    74→}
    75→

entries.detail:
1→{
     2→  "feature": "Safety Check-in & Location Sharing",
     3→  "description": "# Safety Check-in & Location Sharing\n\nSafety-focused check-in system with automated check-ins, emergency contacts, real-time location sharing with trusted contacts, check-in scheduling, and missed check-in alerts. Includes emergency SOS functionality and status updates.\n\n## Rationale\nAddresses critical user pain point: 'Safety concerns when traveling solo without trusted contacts knowing your location.' Also addresses location sharing privacy concerns (pain-7-1, pain-7-2) by implementing privacy-first approach. This is a key differentiator from general travel apps.\n\n## User Stories\n- As a solo traveler, I want to share my location with trusted contacts so that someone knows where I am in case of emergency\n- As a safety-conscious user, I want automated check-ins so that my contacts are alerted if I don't report in safely\n- As a traveler, I want an emergency SOS button so that I can quickly get help if needed\n- As a privacy-conscious user, I want to control exactly who sees my location and when\n\n## Acceptance Criteria\n- [ ] Users can designate trusted contacts from their phone or app community\n- [ ] Users can schedule automatic check-ins at specific times or locations\n- [ ] Trusted contacts receive location updates and status notifications\n- [ ] Missed check-ins trigger alerts to trusted contacts with last known location\n- [ ] Emergency SOS button shares real-time location with all trusted contacts\n- [ ] Users can manually update their status (safe, need help, emergency)\n- [ ] Location sharing is granular - users control what, when, and with whom\n- [ ] Battery-efficient location tracking prevents excessive drain\n",

entries.content:
Perfect! The implementation is complete. Here's a summary of what was accomplished:

## Implementation Summary

### ✅ Subtask 1.4 Completed: Create use cases for trusted contacts

I successfully implemented four use case classes for managing trusted contacts:

#### **Files Created:**
1. **`add_trusted_contact.dart`** (577 bytes)
   - `AddTrustedContactUseCase` - Adds new trusted contacts
   - Delegates to `SafetyRepository.addTrustedContact()`

2. **`remove_trusted_contact.dart`** (592 bytes)
   - `RemoveTrustedContactUseCase` - Removes existing trusted contacts
   - Delegates to `SafetyRepository.removeTrustedContact()`

3. **`update_trusted_contact.dart`** (1.5 KB)
   - `UpdateTrustedContactUseCase` - Updates contact details
   - Includes helper methods for updating location sharing permissions
   - Includes helper methods for updating notification preferences

4. **`get_trusted_contacts.dart`** (877 bytes)
   - `GetTrustedContactsUseCase` - Retrieves all trusted contacts
   - Includes `getContactById()` method for fetching specific contacts

#### **Key Features:**
- ✅ Follows established code patterns from auth and profile features
- ✅ Uses const constructors for better performance
- ✅ Comprehensive documentation with Dart doc comments
- ✅ Clean delegation to SafetyRepository interface
- ✅ No console.log or debugging statements
- ✅ Error handling delegated to repository layer

#### **Commits:**
1. `c88b399` - Initial implementation of use cases
2. `479908d` - Updated plan and progress files

The implementation is ready and follows all project conventions. The next step would be **Subtask 1.5: Create use cases for check-ins**.
I'll help you implement the use cases for manual and scheduled check-ins. Let me start by understanding the current state of the project.

entries.detail:
1→import 'package:freezed_annotation/freezed_annotation.dart';
     2→
     3→part 'check_in.freezed.dart';
     4→part 'check_in.g.dart';
     5→
     6→/// Status of a check-in
     7→enum CheckInStatus {
     8→  /// Check-in is scheduled for the future
     9→  scheduled,
    10→  /// Check-in is currently active (waiting for user to check in)
    11→  active,
    12→  /// Check-in was completed successfully
    13→  completed,
    14→  /// Check-in was missed (user didn't check in on time)
    15→  missed,
    16→  /// Check-in was cancelled
    17→  cancelled,
    18→}
    19→
    20→/// Type of check-in trigger
    21→enum CheckInTriggerType {
    22→  /// Manual check-in initiated by user
    23→  manual,
    24→  /// Scheduled check-in at a specific time
    25→  scheduledTime,
    26→  /// Location-based check-in when arriving at a location
    27→  locationArrival,
    28→  /// Location-based check-in when departing a location
    29→  locationDeparture,
    30→}
    31→
    32→/// Represents a check-in (manual or scheduled)
    33→@freezed
    34→class CheckIn with _$CheckIn {
    35→  const factory CheckIn({
    36→    /// Unique identifier for the check-in
    37→    required String id,
    38→
    39→    /// User ID who created this check-in
    40→    required String userId,
    41→
    42→    /// Type of check-in trigger
    43→    required CheckInTriggerType triggerType,
    44→
    45→    /// Current status of the check-in
    46→    required CheckInStatus status,
    47→
    48→    /// Scheduled time for the check-in (null for manual)
    49→    DateTime? scheduledTime,
    50→
    51→    /// Deadline for completing the check-in
    52→    DateTime? deadline,
    53→
    54→    /// When the check-in was actually completed
    55→    DateTime? completedAt,
    56→
    57→    /// Location data at check-in time
    58→    CheckInLocation? location,
    59→
    60→    /// User's status message at check-in
    61→    String? statusMessage,
    62→
    63→    /// Associated trip ID (if check-in is part of a trip)
    64→    String? tripId,
    65→
    66→    /// IDs of trusted contacts to notify
    67→    required List<String> notifyContactIds,
    68→
    69→    /// Whether alert was sent to contacts for missed check-in
    70→    @Default(false) bool alertSent,
    71→
    72→    /// When alert was sent (if applicable)
    73→    DateTime? alertSentAt,
    74→
    75→    /// Additional metadata
    76→    Map<String, dynamic>? metadata,
    77→
    78→    /// When this check-in was created
    79→    required DateTime createdAt,
    80→
    81→    /// When this check-in was last updated
    82→    DateTime? updatedAt,
    83→  }) = _CheckIn;
    84→
    85→  factory CheckIn.fromJson(Map<String, dynamic> json) =>
    86→      _$CheckInFromJson(json);
    87→}
    88→
    89→/// Location data associated with a check-in
    90→@freezed
    91→class CheckInLocation with _$CheckInLocation {
    92→  const factory CheckInLocation({
    93→    /// Latitude
    94→    required double latitude,
    95→
    96→    /// Longitude
    97→    required double longitude,
    98→
    99→    /// Accuracy of the location in meters
   100→    double? accuracy,
   101→
   102→    /// Altitude in meters
   103→    double? altitude,
   104→
   105→    /// Human-readable address
   106→    String? address,
   107→
   108→    /// Place name (if applicable)
   109→    String? placeName,
   110→
   111→    /// When this location was recorded
   112→    required DateTime timestamp,
   113→  }) = _CheckInLocation;
   114→
   115→  factory CheckInLocation.fromJson(Map<String, dynamic> json) =>
   116→      _$CheckInLocationFromJson(json);
   117→}
   118→

1→import '../entities/trusted_contact.dart';
     2→import '../repositories/safety_repository.dart';
     3→
     4→/// Use case for updating an existing trusted contact
     5→class UpdateTrustedContactUseCase {
     6→  final SafetyRepository _repository;
     7→
     8→  /// Creates a new [UpdateTrustedContactUseCase] with the given repository
     9→  const UpdateTrustedContactUseCase(this._repository);
    10→
    11→  /// Execute the use case to update a trusted contact
    12→  ///
    13→  /// Updates the trusted contact with new information. The contact must exist
    14→  /// and the [contact.id] must match an existing trusted contact.
    15→  /// Returns the updated [TrustedContact].
    16→  Future<TrustedContact> call(TrustedContact contact) =>
    17→      _repository.updateTrustedContact(contact);
    18→
    19→  /// Update location sharing permission for a contact
    20→  ///
    21→  /// Enables or disables location sharing with the specified contact.
    22→  Future<void> updateLocationSharing({
    23→    required String contactId,
    24→    required bool enabled,
    25→  }) =>
    26→      _repository.updateLocationSharingPermission(
    27→        contactId: contactId,
    28→        enabled: enabled,
    29→      );
    30→
    31→  /// Update notification preferences for a contact
    32→  ///
    33→  /// Controls whether the contact receives check-ins and/or emergency alerts.
    34→  Future<void> updateNotificationPreferences({
    35→    required String contactId,
    36→    required bool receivesCheckIns,
    37→    required bool receivesEmergencyAlerts,
    38→  }) =>
    39→      _repository.updateContactNotificationPreferences(
    40→        contactId: contactId,
    41→        receivesCheckIns: receivesCheckIns,
    42→        receivesEmergencyAlerts: receivesEmergencyAlerts,
    43→      );
    44→}
    45→

entries.content:
Perfect! Let me provide a summary of what was completed:

## ✅ Subtask 1.5 Completed Successfully!

### What Was Implemented

I've successfully implemented **5 use cases for manual and scheduled check-ins** following the established patterns in the codebase:

#### 1. **CreateCheckInUseCase** (`create_check_in.dart`)
- Creates new manual or scheduled check-ins
- Delegates to `SafetyRepository.createCheckIn()`
- Returns the created `CheckIn` with generated ID and timestamps

#### 2. **CompleteCheckInUseCase** (`complete_check_in.dart`)
- Completes check-ins with location data and optional status message
- Named parameters for checkInId, location, and optional statusMessage
- Returns updated `CheckIn` with status set to `CheckInStatus.completed`

#### 3. **ScheduleCheckInUseCase** (`schedule_check_in.dart`)
- Schedules automatic check-ins with deadline and notifications
- Supports all scheduling options: userId, scheduledTime, deadline, location, statusMessage, notifyContactIds, tripId, triggerType
- Returns created `CheckIn` with status set to `CheckInStatus.scheduled`

#### 4. **CancelCheckInUseCase** (`cancel_check_in.dart`)
- Cancels scheduled check-ins
- Simple interface taking just the checkInId
- Delegates to `SafetyRepository.cancelCheckIn()`

#### 5. **GetUpcomingCheckInsUseCase** (`get_upcoming_check_ins.dart`)
- Retrieves upcoming check-ins (main `call()` method)
- Includes helper methods:
  - `getAllCheckIns()` - Get all check-ins regardless of status
  - `getCheckInsByTrip(tripId)` - Get check-ins for a specific trip
  - `getCheckInById(checkInId)` - Get a specific check-in by ID

### Quality Verification

✅ **Follows established patterns** - All use cases match the pattern from trusted contacts use cases  
✅ **Proper documentation** - Comprehensive comments explaining purpose and behavior  
✅ **No console.log statements** - Clean production-ready code  
✅ **Error handling** - Delegates to repository which will handle errors  
✅ **Type safety** - Proper use of nullable and required parameters  

### Commits

1. **Commit 1abdb27**: Implementation of all 5 check-in use cases
2. **Commit cfcceec**: Updated implementation plan and build progress documentation

### Next Steps

The next subtask to implement is **1.6: Create use cases for location sharing**, which will include:
- `ShareLocationUseCase`
- `StopLocationSharingUseCase`
- `GetActiveLocationSharesUseCase`

All files are in `lib/features/safety/domain/usecases/` and ready for use! 🎉
I'll help you implement the use cases for sharing location with trusted contacts. Let me start by understanding the context and current state.

entries.detail:
1→# Safety Check-in & Location Sharing
     2→
     3→Safety-focused check-in system with automated check-ins, emergency contacts, real-time location sharing with trusted contacts, check-in scheduling, and missed check-in alerts. Includes emergency SOS functionality and status updates.
     4→
     5→## Rationale
     6→Addresses critical user pain point: 'Safety concerns when traveling solo without trusted contacts knowing your location.' Also addresses location sharing privacy concerns (pain-7-1, pain-7-2) by implementing privacy-first approach. This is a key differentiator from general travel apps.
     7→
     8→## User Stories
     9→- As a solo traveler, I want to share my location with trusted contacts so that someone knows where I am in case of emergency
    10→- As a safety-conscious user, I want automated check-ins so that my contacts are alerted if I don't report in safely
    11→- As a traveler, I want an emergency SOS button so that I can quickly get help if needed
    12→- As a privacy-conscious user, I want to control exactly who sees my location and when
    13→
    14→## Acceptance Criteria
    15→- [ ] Users can designate trusted contacts from their phone or app community
    16→- [ ] Users can schedule automatic check-ins at specific times or locations
    17→- [ ] Trusted contacts receive location updates and status notifications
    18→- [ ] Missed check-ins trigger alerts to trusted contacts with last known location
    19→- [ ] Emergency SOS button shares real-time location with all trusted contacts
    20→- [ ] Users can manually update their status (safe, need help, emergency)
    21→- [ ] Location sharing is granular - users control what, when, and with whom
    22→- [ ] Battery-efficient location tracking prevents excessive drain
    23→

1→import 'package:freezed_annotation/freezed_annotation.dart';
     2→
     3→part 'location_update.freezed.dart';
     4→part 'location_update.g.dart';
     5→
     6→/// Status of location sharing
     7→enum LocationSharingStatus {
     8→  /// Location sharing is active
     9→  active,
    10→  /// Location sharing is paused
    11→  paused,
    12→  /// Location sharing has ended
    13→  ended,
    14→}
    15→
    16→/// Represents a location update shared with trusted contacts
    17→@freezed
    18→class LocationUpdate with _$LocationUpdate {
    19→  const factory LocationUpdate({
    20→    /// Unique identifier for the location update
    21→    required String id,
    22→
    23→    /// User ID who is sharing their location
    24→    required String userId,
    25→
    26→    /// Latitude
    27→    required double latitude,
    28→
    29→    /// Longitude
    30→    required double longitude,
    31→
    32→    /// Accuracy of the location in meters
    33→    double? accuracy,
    34→
    35→    /// Altitude in meters
    36→    double? altitude,
    37→
    38→    /// Speed in m/s
    39→    double? speed,
    40→
    41→    /// Heading in degrees
    42→    double? heading,
    43→
    44→    /// Human-readable address
    45→    String? address,
    46→
    47→    /// Place name (if applicable)
    48→    String? placeName,
    49→
    50→    /// Status of location sharing
    51→    required LocationSharingStatus sharingStatus,
    52→
    53→    /// IDs of trusted contacts receiving this update
    54→    required List<String> sharedWithContactIds,
    55→
    56→    /// Battery level at time of update (0-100)
    57→    int? batteryLevel,
    58→
    59→    /// Whether this is an emergency location update
    60→    @Default(false) bool isEmergency,
    61→
    62→    /// Associated emergency/SOS alert ID (if applicable)
    63→    String? emergencyAlertId,
    64→
    65→    /// Associated check-in ID (if applicable)
    66→    String? checkInId,
    67→
    68→    /// Additional metadata
    69→    Map<String, dynamic>? metadata,
    70→
    71→    /// When this location update was created
    72→    required DateTime createdAt,
    73→  }) = _LocationUpdate;
    74→
    75→  factory LocationUpdate.fromJson(Map<String, dynamic> json) =>
    76→      _$LocationUpdateFromJson(json);
    77→}

    11→  /// Execute the use case to share location with trusted contacts
    12→  ///
    13→  /// Returns the created [LocationUpdate] containing the shared location details.
    14→  /// The location can be shared as a normal update or as an emergency update.
    15→  Future<LocationUpdate> call({
    16→    required double latitude,
    17→    required double longitude,
    18→    double? accuracy,
    19→    double? altitude,
    20→    double? speed,
    21→    double? heading,
    22→    String? address,
    23→    String? placeName,
    24→    required List<String> shareWithContactIds,
    25→    int? batteryLevel,
    26→    bool isEmergency = false,
    27→    String? emergencyAlertId,
    28→    String? checkInId,
    29→  }) async {
    30→    return _repository.shareLocation(
    31→      latitude: latitude,
    32→      longitude: longitude,
    33→      accuracy: accuracy,
    34→      altitude: altitude,
    35→      speed: speed,
    36→      heading: heading,
    37→      address: address,
    38→      placeName: placeName,
    39→      shareWithContactIds: shareWithContactIds,
    40→      batteryLevel: batteryLevel,
    41→      isEmergency: isEmergency,
    42→      emergencyAlertId: emergencyAlertId,
    43→      checkInId: checkInId,
    44→    );
    45→  }
    46→
    47→  /// Share location with a single trusted contact
    48→  ///
    49→  /// Convenience method for sharing with a single contact.
    50→  /// Returns the created [LocationUpdate].
    51→  Future<LocationUpdate> shareWithContact({
    52→    required double latitude,

    54→    required String contactId,

    56→    double? altitude,

    72→      heading: heading,
    73→      address: address,
    74→      placeName: placeName,
    75→      shareWithContactIds: [contactId],
    76→      batteryLevel: batteryLevel,
    77→      isEmergency: isEmergency,
    78→      emergencyAlertId: emergencyAlertId,
    79→      checkInId: checkInId,
    80→    );
    81→  }
    82→
    83→  /// Share emergency location with all trusted contacts
    84→  ///
    85→  /// Convenience method for emergency situations that shares location

    87→  /// Returns the created [LocationUpdate].
    88→  Future<LocationUpdate> shareEmergencyLocation({

entries.detail:
1→# Safety Check-in & Location Sharing - Build Progress
     2→
     3→## Project Status: IN PROGRESS
     4→
     5→Implementation of Phase 1 has begun.
     6→
     7→## Completed Subtasks
     8→
     9→### Phase 1: Core Data Models & Domain Layer
    10→
    11→✅ **Subtask 1.1: Create domain entities** (COMPLETED)
    12→- Created TrustedContact entity with enums for ContactSource and ContactPermission
    13→- Created CheckIn entity with nested CheckInLocation and enums for CheckInStatus and CheckInTriggerType
    14→- Created LocationUpdate entity with enum for LocationSharingStatus
    15→- Created SafetyAlert entity with nested SafetyAlertLocation and enums for SafetyAlertType and SafetyAlertStatus
    16→- Created SafetyStatus entity with nested SafetyStatusLocation and enum for SafetyStatusType
    17→- All entities use Freezed for immutability and code generation
    18→- Entities include comprehensive documentation and support JSON serialization
    19→- Commit: c2cf812
    20→
    21→✅ **Subtask 1.2: Create data models for serialization** (COMPLETED)
    22→- Created TrustedContactModel with enum serialization (ContactSource, ContactPermission)
    23→- Created CheckInModel with nested CheckInLocationModel
    24→- Created LocationUpdateModel with sharing status enum serialization
    25→- Created SafetyAlertModel with nested SafetyAlertLocationModel
    26→- Created SafetyStatusModel with nested SafetyStatusLocationModel
    27→- All models extend their corresponding domain entities
    28→- Models handle snake_case to camelCase conversion for JSON serialization
    29→- Include comprehensive fromJson/toJson, fromEntity/toEntity methods
    30→- Commit: 4e243e9
    31→
    32→✅ **Subtask 1.3: Define repository interfaces** (COMPLETED)
    33→- Created SafetyRepository interface with comprehensive safety operations
    34→- Trusted contacts: add, remove, update, get, list
    35→- Check-ins: create, complete, schedule, cancel, get (all, upcoming, by trip), update status
    36→- Location sharing: share, stop (specific/all), get active shares, get updates, update permissions
    37→- Emergency SOS: trigger SOS, update safety status, get safety status (current/user)
    38→- Safety alerts: get (all, by ID, recent, missed check-in), acknowledge, resolve, cancel
    39→- Battery monitoring: update level, get current level
    40→- Settings: update contact notification preferences, get/update safety settings
    41→- All methods return Future types for async operations
    42→- Follows existing repository patterns from auth and profile features
    43→- Commit: 5c047e6
    44→
    45→✅ **Subtask 1.4: Create use cases for trusted contacts** (COMPLETED)
    46→- Created AddTrustedContactUseCase for adding new trusted contacts
    47→- Created RemoveTrustedContactUseCase for removing existing contacts
    48→- Created UpdateTrustedContactUseCase for updating contact details with helper methods for location sharing and notification preferences
    49→- Created GetTrustedContactsUseCase for retrieving all contacts or getting a specific contact by ID
    50→- All use cases follow established patterns with const constructors and proper documentation
    51→- Use cases delegate to SafetyRepository interface methods
    52→- Commit: c88b399
    53→
    54→✅ **Subtask 1.5: Create use cases for check-ins** (COMPLETED)
    55→- Created CreateCheckInUseCase for creating new manual or scheduled check-ins
    56→- Created CompleteCheckInUseCase for completing check-ins with location and optional status message
    57→- Created ScheduleCheckInUseCase for scheduling automatic check-ins with deadline and notifications
    58→- Created CancelCheckInUseCase for canceling scheduled check-ins
    59→- Created GetUpcomingCheckInsUseCase for retrieving upcoming, all, and trip-specific check-ins with helper methods
    60→- All use cases follow established patterns with const constructors and proper documentation
    61→- Use cases delegate to SafetyRepository interface methods
    62→- Commit: 1abdb27
    63→
    64→## Next Steps
    65→
    66→1. **Subtask 1.6**: Create use cases for location sharing
    67→2. Implement ShareLocation, StopLocationSharing, GetActiveLocationShares
    68→
    69→### Phases Overview
    70→

entries.content: Excellent! Let me update the todo list to mark everything as completed:

Perfect! Subtask 1.6 has been successfully completed. Here's a summary of what was implemented:

## ✅ Subtask 1.6: Create use cases for location sharing - COMPLETED

### Implementation Summary

Created three use cases for location sharing with trusted contacts:

**1. ShareLocationUseCase** (`lib/features/safety/domain/usecases/share_location.dart`)
- Main `call()` method for sharing location with multiple contacts
- `shareWithContact()` convenience method for single contact sharing
- `shareEmergencyLocation()` convenience method for emergency situations
- Supports comprehensive location data (coordinates, accuracy, altitude, speed, heading)
- Handles additional context (address, place name, battery level)
- Links to emergency alerts or check-ins when applicable

**2. StopLocationSharingUseCase** (`lib/features/safety/domain/usecases/stop_location_sharing.dart`)
- Main `call()` method to stop sharing with specific contacts
- `stopWithContact()` convenience method for single contact
- `stopAll()` method to stop all location sharing

**3. GetActiveLocationSharesUseCase** (`lib/features/safety/domain/usecases/get_active_location_shares.dart`)
- Main `call()` method to get all active location shares
- `getRecentUpdates()` for recent updates with optional date filtering
- `getSharesForContact()` to filter by specific contact
- `getEmergencyShares()` to get only emergency updates
- `getSharesForCheckIn()` to filter by check-in ID
- `getSharesForEmergencyAlert()` to filter by emergency alert ID

### Quality Checklist ✅
- ✅ Follows existing code patterns from trusted contacts and check-ins use cases
- ✅ Uses const constructors and proper documentation
- ✅ Delegates to SafetyRepository interface methods
- ✅ Includes convenience helper methods for common operations
- ✅ No console.log or debugging statements
- ✅ Clean commit with descriptive message
- ✅ Implementation plan and build progress updated

### Commits
- **d27e4d4**: Initial implementation of location sharing use cases
- **ad30d35**: Updated plan and progress for subtask completion

### Next Steps
The next subtask (1.7) is to create use cases for emergency SOS functionality, which will include:
- TriggerEmergencySOS use case
- UpdateSafetyStatus use case  
- GetSafetyStatus use case

All code follows Clean Architecture principles and integrates seamlessly with the existing SafetyRepository interface.
I'll help you implement the emergency SOS functionality. Let me start by reading the specification and plan files to understand what needs to be done.

1→import 'package:freezed_annotation/freezed_annotation.dart';
     2→
     3→part 'safety_status.freezed.dart';
     4→part 'safety_status.g.dart';
     5→
     6→/// Current safety status of the user
     7→enum SafetyStatusType {
     8→  /// User is safe
     9→  safe,
    10→  /// User needs help but it's not an emergency
    11→  needHelp,
    12→  /// User is in an emergency situation
    13→  emergency,
    14→  /// Unknown status
    15→  unknown,
    16→}
    17→
    18→/// Represents the user's current safety status
    19→@freezed
    20→class SafetyStatus with _$SafetyStatus {
    21→  const factory SafetyStatus({
    22→    /// Unique identifier for the safety status
    23→    required String id,
    24→
    25→    /// User ID
    26→    required String userId,
    27→
    28→    /// Current safety status
    29→    required SafetyStatusType status,
    30→
    31→    /// Optional message describing the status
    32→    String? message,
    33→
    34→    /// Location associated with this status
    35→    SafetyStatusLocation? location,
    36→
    37→    /// Battery level at time of status update (0-100)
    38→    int? batteryLevel,
    39→
    40→    /// When this status was set
    41→    required DateTime timestamp,
    42→
    43→    /// When this status was last updated
    44→    DateTime? updatedAt,
    45→
    46→    /// Associated safety alert ID (if status was set via alert)
    47→    String? safetyAlertId,
    48→
    49→    /// Associated check-in ID (if status was set via check-in)
    50→    String? checkInId,
    51→
    52→    /// Additional metadata
    53→    Map<String, dynamic>? metadata,
    54→  }) = _SafetyStatus;
    55→
    56→  factory SafetyStatus.fromJson(Map<String, dynamic> json) =>
    57→      _$SafetyStatusFromJson(json);
    58→}
    59→
    60→/// Location data associated with a safety status
    61→@freezed
    62→class SafetyStatusLocation with _$SafetyStatusLocation {
    63→  const factory SafetyStatusLocation({
    64→    /// Latitude
    65→    required double latitude,
    66→
    67→    /// Longitude
    68→    required double longitude,
    69→
    70→    /// Accuracy of the location in meters
    71→    double? accuracy,
    72→
    73→    /// Altitude in meters
    74→    double? altitude,
    75→
    76→    /// Human-readable address
    77→    String? address,
    78→
    79→    /// Place name (if applicable)
    80→    String? placeName,
    81→
    82→    /// When this location was recorded
    83→    required DateTime timestamp,
    84→  }) = _SafetyStatusLocation;
    85→
    86→  factory SafetyStatusLocation.fromJson(Map<String, dynamic> json) =>
    87→      _$SafetyStatusLocationFromJson(json);
    88→}
    89→

1→import '../entities/safety_alert.dart';
     2→import '../repositories/safety_repository.dart';
     3→
     4→/// Use case for triggering an emergency SOS alert
     5→class TriggerEmergencySOSUseCase {
     6→  final SafetyRepository _repository;
     7→
     8→  /// Creates a new [TriggerEmergencySOSUseCase] with the given repository
     9→  const TriggerEmergencySOSUseCase(this._repository);
    10→
    11→  /// Execute the use case to trigger an emergency SOS alert
    12→  ///
    13→  /// Returns the created [SafetyAlert] containing the alert details.
    14→  /// This will notify all trusted contacts and share the user's current location.
    15→  ///
    16→  /// The [location] parameter is required and should contain the user's current
    17→  /// GPS coordinates. Optional [message] can be provided to give additional context
    18→  /// about the emergency. [notifyContactIds] specifies which trusted contacts
    19→  /// should be notified - if not provided, all trusted contacts will be notified.
    20→  ///
    21→  /// The [batteryLevel] can be optionally provided to help contacts understand
    22→  /// how long the user's device will remain operational. [tripId] can be associated
    23→  /// if the emergency is related to a specific trip.
    24→  Future<SafetyAlert> call({
    25→    required String userId,
    26→    String? message,
    27→    required SafetyAlertLocation location,
    28→    required List<String> notifyContactIds,
    29→    int? batteryLevel,
    30→    String? tripId,
    31→  }) async {
    32→    return _repository.triggerEmergencySOS(
    33→      userId: userId,
    34→      message: message,
    35→      location: location,
    36→      notifyContactIds: notifyContactIds,
    37→      batteryLevel: batteryLevel,
    38→      tripId: tripId,
    39→    );
    40→  }
    41→}
    42→

1→{
     2→  "feature": "Safety Check-in & Location Sharing",
     3→  "description": "# Safety Check-in & Location Sharing\n\nSafety-focused check-in system with automated check-ins, emergency contacts, real-time location sharing with trusted contacts, check-in scheduling, and missed check-in alerts. Includes emergency SOS functionality and status updates.\n\n## Rationale\nAddresses critical user pain point: 'Safety concerns when traveling solo without trusted contacts knowing your location.' Also addresses location sharing privacy concerns (pain-7-1, pain-7-2) by implementing privacy-first approach. This is a key differentiator from general travel apps.\n\n## User Stories\n- As a solo traveler, I want to share my location with trusted contacts so that someone knows where I am in case of emergency\n- As a safety-conscious user, I want automated check-ins so that my contacts are alerted if I don't report in safely\n- As a traveler, I want an emergency SOS button so that I can quickly get help if needed\n- As a privacy-conscious user, I want to control exactly who sees my location and when\n\n## Acceptance Criteria\n- [ ] Users can designate trusted contacts from their phone or app community\n- [ ] Users can schedule automatic check-ins at specific times or locations\n- [ ] Trusted contacts receive location updates and status notifications\n- [ ] Missed check-ins trigger alerts to trusted contacts with last known location\n- [ ] Emergency SOS button shares real-time location with all trusted contacts\n- [ ] Users can manually update their status (safe, need help, emergency)\n- [ ] Location sharing is granular - users control what, when, and with whom\n- [ ] Battery-efficient location tracking prevents excessive drain\n",

entries.content:
## ✅ Subtask 1.7 Completed Successfully!

I've successfully implemented all three use cases for emergency SOS functionality. Here's what was accomplished:

### 📁 Files Created

1. **`lib/features/safety/domain/usecases/trigger_emergency_sos.dart`**
   - Triggers emergency SOS alerts to notify trusted contacts
   - Includes user's location, optional message, and battery level
   - Returns created SafetyAlert with all alert details

2. **`lib/features/safety/domain/usecases/update_safety_status.dart`**
   - Main `call()` method for setting any status type
   - Three convenience methods: `markAsSafe()`, `markAsNeedHelp()`, `markAsEmergency()`
   - Supports optional message, location, battery level, and associations with alerts/check-ins

3. **`lib/features/safety/domain/usecases/get_safety_status.dart`**
   - `call()` method retrieves current user's status
   - `forUser()` helper method retrieves status for specific users
   - Useful for both self-status checks and trusted contact monitoring

### ✨ Quality Checklist

- ✅ Follows established patterns from existing use cases
- ✅ All use cases have const constructors
- ✅ Comprehensive documentation with dartdoc comments
- ✅ Proper delegation to SafetyRepository interface
- ✅ No console.log or debugging statements
- ✅ Async operations with Future return types
- ✅ No analysis errors

### 📊 Phase 1 Status: **COMPLETE**

**Phase 1: Core Data Models & Domain Layer** (7/7 subtasks completed)
- ✅ 1.1: Domain entities
- ✅ 1.2: Data models for serialization
- ✅ 1.3: Repository interfaces
- ✅ 1.4: Use cases for trusted contacts
- ✅ 1.5: Use cases for check-ins
- ✅ 1.6: Use cases for location sharing
- ✅ 1.7: Use cases for emergency SOS

### 🔄 Git Commits

- **657b828**: Initial commit with the three use case implementations
- **8f932f1**: Documentation update marking subtask 1.7 as completed

### 🎯 Next Steps

**Phase 2: Data Layer Implementation** - Ready to begin with:
- Subtask 2.1: Create safety-specific exceptions
- Subtask 2.2: Implement local data source
- Subtask 2.3: Implement remote data source
- Subtask 2.4: Implement repository
- Subtask 2.5: Add GraphQL mutations and queries

The domain layer is now complete and ready for the data layer implementation!
I'll help you implement this subtask. Let me start by reading the key files to understand the context and requirements.

    26→/// Base class for all exceptions in the app

    28→  final String message;

    31→
    32→  @override
    33→  String toString() => message;
    34→}
    35→
    36→/// Exception thrown when there is a server error
    37→class ServerException extends AppException {
    38→  final int statusCode;
    39→
    40→  ServerException({
    41→    required String message,
    42→    required this.statusCode,
    43→  }) : super(message);
    44→}
    45→
    46→/// Exception thrown when there is an authentication error
    47→class AuthException extends AppException {
    48→  final AuthErrorType type;
    49→
    50→  AuthException({
    51→    required String message,
    52→    required this.type,
    53→  }) : super(message);
    54→}

entries.content:
Perfect! Let me create a summary of the completed work:

## Summary

I've successfully completed **Subtask 2.1: Create safety exceptions** for the Safety Check-in & Location Sharing feature.

### What Was Implemented

Created a comprehensive safety exceptions library at `lib/core/error/safety_exceptions.dart` with **30+ exception classes** organized by feature area:

1. **Trusted Contact Exceptions** (5 exceptions)
   - TrustedContactNotFoundException
   - TrustedContactAlreadyExistsException
   - InvalidContactInformationException
   - TrustedContactLimitExceededException
   - NoNotificationMethodException

2. **Check-in Exceptions** (6 exceptions)
   - CheckInNotFoundException
   - CheckInAlreadyCompletedException
   - CheckInOverdueException (with DateTime field)
   - InvalidCheckInScheduleException
   - CheckInCreationFailedException
   - CheckInCannotBeCanceledException

3. **Location Sharing Exceptions** (6 exceptions)
   - LocationServiceUnavailableException
   - LocationPermissionDeniedException (with permanent flag)
   - InvalidLocationDataException
   - LocationSharingNotActiveException
   - LocationSharingFailedException
   - LocationUpdateFailedException

4. **Emergency SOS Exceptions** (3 exceptions)
   - EmergencySOSAlreadyActiveException
   - EmergencySOSTriggerFailedException
   - NoTrustedContactsConfiguredException

5. **Safety Alert Exceptions** (4 exceptions)
   - SafetyAlertNotFoundException
   - SafetyAlertAlreadyAcknowledgedException
   - SafetyAlertAlreadyResolvedException
   - SafetyAlertCreationFailedException

6. **Background & Notification Exceptions** (5 exceptions)
   - NotificationPermissionDeniedException
   - NotificationSchedulingFailedException
   - BackgroundTaskRegistrationFailedException
   - BackgroundTaskInitializationFailedException
   - MissedCheckInDetectionFailedException

7. **Battery & Power Exceptions** (2 exceptions)
   - BatteryCriticallyLowException (with batteryLevel field)
   - BatteryMonitoringUnavailableException

8. **Settings & Configuration Exceptions** (3 exceptions)
   - InvalidSafetySettingsException
   - SafetySettingsSaveFailedException
   - SafetySettingsLoadFailedException

9. **Cache & Storage Exceptions** (2 exceptions)
   - SafetyCacheException
   - SafetyCacheRetrievalException

10. **Network & Sync Exceptions** (2 exceptions)
    - SafetySyncFailedException
    - SafetyOfflineException

### Quality Assurance

✅ **Follows project patterns**: All exceptions extend `AppException` from the core errors module
✅ **Consistent structure**: Const constructors with optional message parameter and unique error codes
✅ **Comprehensive documentation**: Every exception has descriptive documentation comments
✅ **Type safety**: Exceptions with additional data (like `CheckInOverdueException` and `BatteryCriticallyLowException`) have custom `toString()` methods
✅ **Clean code**: No debugging statements, properly organized by feature area
✅ **Committed**: Clean git commit with detailed message explaining all exceptions

### Files Modified

- Created: `lib/core/error/safety_exceptions.dart` (313 lines)
- Updated: `.auto-claude/specs/004-safety-check-in-location-sharing/implementation_plan.json` (marked subtask 2.1 as completed)
- Updated: `.auto-claude/specs/004-safety-check-in-location-sharing/build-progress.txt` (added completion details)

### Commit

Commit: `8d5d3a2` - "auto-claude: 2.1 - Define safety-specific exceptions for error handling"

The safety exceptions infrastructure is now ready to be used throughout the data layer implementation, providing robust error handling for all safety feature operations.
I'll help you create the SafetyLocalDataSource for caching safety data using SharedPreferences. Let me start by reading the key files to understand the requirements and existing patterns.

    11→    required super.source,

    13→    required super.permission,

    21→  });
    22→
    23→  /// Creates a [TrustedContactModel] from JSON map

entries.content:
Excellent! The subtask has been completed successfully. Let me provide a summary:

## ✅ Subtask 2.2 Completed: SafetyLocalDataSource Implementation

I've successfully created the SafetyLocalDataSource for caching safety data using SharedPreferences. Here's what was implemented:

### Files Created:
1. **`lib/features/safety/data/datasources/safety_local_data_source.dart`** - Interface defining all local data operations
2. **`lib/features/safety/data/datasources/safety_local_data_source_impl.dart`** - Implementation using SharedPreferences

### Features Implemented:

**Trusted Contacts Operations:**
- Cache/retrieve list of trusted contacts
- Cache/retrieve individual contact by ID
- Remove cached contact

**Check-ins Operations:**
- Cache/retrieve all check-ins
- Get upcoming check-ins (with filtering)
- Cache/retrieve individual check-in by ID
- Remove cached check-in

**Location Updates Operations:**
- Cache/retrieve all location updates
- Get active location shares (with status filtering)
- Cache/retrieve individual location update

**Safety Alerts Operations:**
- Cache/retrieve all safety alerts
- Get recent alerts (with sorting and limiting)
- Get missed check-in alerts
- Cache/retrieve individual alert by ID

**Safety Status Operations:**
- Cache/retrieve current safety status

**Battery & Settings Operations:**
- Cache/retrieve battery level
- Cache/retrieve safety settings

**Cache Management:**
- Cache expiration (1 hour for safety-critical data)
- Clear all cached safety data
- Get last cache update timestamp

### Key Design Decisions:
- ✅ Follows established patterns from auth and profile features
- ✅ Uses SharedPreferences for local storage
- ✅ Implements comprehensive error handling with SafetyCacheException
- ✅ Short cache duration (1 hour) for safety-critical data
- ✅ Automatic timestamp tracking for cache expiration
- ✅ JSON serialization/deserialization for all model types

### Git Commits:
- **6193278** - Initial implementation of SafetyLocalDataSource
- **3e73b8d** - Updated implementation plan (marked subtask 2.2 as completed)

The implementation is ready for use in the SafetyRepository and follows all project conventions and patterns.
I'll help you implement the SafetyRemoteDataSource for API communication. Let me start by reading the specification and plan to understand the requirements.

entries.detail:
1→# Safety Check-in & Location Sharing
     2→
     3→Safety-focused check-in system with automated check-ins, emergency contacts, real-time location sharing with trusted contacts, check-in scheduling, and missed check-in alerts. Includes emergency SOS functionality and status updates.
     4→
     5→## Rationale
     6→Addresses critical user pain point: 'Safety concerns when traveling solo without trusted contacts knowing your location.' Also addresses location sharing privacy concerns (pain-7-1, pain-7-2) by implementing privacy-first approach. This is a key differentiator from general travel apps.
     7→
     8→## User Stories
     9→- As a solo traveler, I want to share my location with trusted contacts so that someone knows where I am in case of emergency
    10→- As a safety-conscious user, I want automated check-ins so that my contacts are alerted if I don't report in safely
    11→- As a traveler, I want an emergency SOS button so that I can quickly get help if needed
    12→- As a privacy-conscious user, I want to control exactly who sees my location and when
    13→
    14→## Acceptance Criteria
    15→- [ ] Users can designate trusted contacts from their phone or app community
    16→- [ ] Users can schedule automatic check-ins at specific times or locations
    17→- [ ] Trusted contacts receive location updates and status notifications
    18→- [ ] Missed check-ins trigger alerts to trusted contacts with last known location
    19→- [ ] Emergency SOS button shares real-time location with all trusted contacts
    20→- [ ] Users can manually update their status (safe, need help, emergency)
    21→- [ ] Location sharing is granular - users control what, when, and with whom
    22→- [ ] Battery-efficient location tracking prevents excessive drain
    23→
